phase1Decision.reason: Story faithfully preserves the original request. Plan is internally consistent with story, matches constitution across all 12 principles, and is complete with payload examples, validation decisions, slice logging, test expectations, and verification scope. No constitution violations, no scope drift, no blocking issues found.
advisoryNotesForCoder: GitLabLabelEventResponse declares 'nested records for user and label': clarify whether user nested record reuses GitLabSingleIssueResponse.GitLabAssigneeDetail or creates a new inline record; both are acceptable but justify the choice if creating a new type to avoid silent duplication

Plan does not specify null-label handling in GitLabLabelEventMapper (GitLab can return null label if label was deleted post-event); Coder should add a null guard and either throw IntegrationException or skip the event — align with constitution Principle 7

Slice 2 says 'map LabelChangeSet list in service' — this should mean IssuesService sets the port-returned List<ChangeSet> directly into EnrichedIssueDetail builder without additional mapping; confirm no mapping logic leaks into the service layer

evidence: Core implementation matches plan: IssuesPort.getLabelEvents added; IssuesService calls both port methods sequentially; GitLabIssuesAdapter.getLabelEvents calls one endpoint (resource_label_events); GitLabLabelEventMapper and GitLabLabelEventResponse added under integration/gitlab/issues/; IssuesResponseMapper maps via instanceof LabelChangeSet pattern in orchestration — all as specified

Karate non-smoke scenario absent: Approved deviation — documented in artifacts/implementation-reports/label-events-api.report.json approvedDeviations and consistent with /memories/session/label-events-api-phase2-brief.md; existing @smoke scenario issues-get-single.feature validates changeSets == '#[]' on GET /api/issues/{issueId} — deviation is justified and recorded
documentation/context-map.md fully updated: all 5 new orchestration model files, 4 new IssueDetailDto inner records, and LabelChangeSetTest.java confirmed present — see docUpdates finding

Principle 1 — dependency arrows inward: orchestration.issues.rest.mapper imports orchestration.issues.model (same layer); integration.gitlab.issues.mapper imports orchestration.issues.model (integration → orchestration contracts is correct) — PASS
Principle 2 — each adapter method maps one external call: getLabelEvents calls only /{issueId}/resource_label_events; getIssueDetail calls only /{issueId} — PASS
Principle 3 — no domain layer changes: all new types in orchestration or integration packages — PASS

Principle 6 — immutability: EnrichedIssueDetail uses List.copyOf(changeSets) in compact constructor (LabelChangeSetTest line 53-74 verified); IssueDetailDto also copies changeSets; all new model types are records — PASS

Principle 7 — no partial failure: IssuesService.getIssueDetail calls getIssueDetail then getLabelEvents with no try-catch; exceptions propagate to centralized handler; component test returns404WhenGitLabReturns404AndDoesNotCallLabelEvents confirms issue-detail failure prevents label-events call — PASS

Principle 8 — explicit mapping at boundaries: GitLabLabelEventMapper (integration/gitlab/issues/mapper/) maps integration DTOs to orchestration ChangeSet; IssuesResponseMapper (orchestration/issues/rest/mapper/) maps orchestration ChangeSet to ChangeSetDto — PASS
GitLabIssuesAdapter: @RequiredArgsConstructor, @Slf4j, @Component; all dependencies final; INFO log before and after getLabelEvents call with issueId and count context — PASS
GitLabLabelEventMapper: @Slf4j, @Component; single toLabelChangeSets method; requiredField helper avoids NPE; @Builder on LabelChangeSet — PASS
New model types: ChangeSet/Change as interfaces; LabelChangeSet/LabelChange/ChangedBy as @Builder records; naming follows '*Set'/change domain vocabulary — PASS
IssueDetailDto: ChangeSetDto as interface; LabelChangeSetDto/ChangedByDto/LabelChangeDto as @Builder records; no numeric suffixes; compact constructor copies changeSets — PASS
IssuesService.getIssueDetail: two distinct port calls, no mapping logic in service (service passes port result directly to EnrichedIssueDetail.builder), INFO logs after both calls — PASS
Null handling: GitLabLabelEventMapper uses requiredField guard which throws IllegalStateException for null fields; centralized exception handler maps to 502 — consistent with existing pattern — PASS
